input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's your complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS:

---

## 1. Xcode (iOS builds)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- **Version discovery:** MAUI release notes specify the minimum Xcode version. Check [learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/whats-new](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/whats-new/) for your target MAUI version.
- After install, run:
  ```
  sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app
  xcodebuild -runFirstLaunch
  sudo xcodebuild -license accept
  ```
- **Verify:** `xcodebuild -version`

## 2. .NET SDK (includes MAUI workload support)

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — get the latest **LTS** or **STS** release depending on your preference.
- **Version discovery:** The MAUI docs list compatible SDK versions. As of .NET 8+, MAUI ships as a workload inside the SDK.
- **Verify:** `dotnet --version`

## 3. .NET MAUI Workload

- Install via the SDK:
  ```
  dotnet workload install maui
  ```
- **Verify:** `dotnet workload list` — should show `maui` (and its sub-workloads like `maui-ios`, `maui-android`).

## 4. Android SDK, Emulator & JDK

The MAUI workload pulls in the **Microsoft OpenJDK** and the required **Android SDK** components automatically when you first build. To manage them explicitly:

- **Android command-line tools:** Bundled via the workload, or install from [developer.android.com/studio#command-tools](https://developer.android.com/studio#command-tools).
- **Android Studio (optional but recommended):** Provides the emulator manager, SDK manager UI, and device tools — [developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio).
- **JDK:** The workload installs Microsoft's OpenJDK build. If you need to override, use [learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download](https://learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download).
- **Verify:**
  ```
  dotnet build -t:InstallAndroidDependencies -f net8.0-android \
    -p:AndroidSdkDirectory="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" \
    -p:AcceptAndroidSDKLicenses=true
  ```
  (Adjust the TFM like `net9.0-android` to match your SDK version.)

## 5. IDE — Pick one

| IDE | Source |
|---|---|
| **Visual Studio Code + .NET MAUI extension** | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) + extension `ms-dotnettools.dotnet-maui` |
| **Visual Studio for Mac** (discontinued after v17.6) | Only if on .NET 7 or earlier |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) — full MAUI support |

VS Code with the C# Dev Kit + MAUI extension is now the primary Microsoft-supported macOS workflow.

## 6. Apple provisioning (for physical iOS devices)

- Register at [developer.apple.com/account](https://developer.apple.com/account) (free tier works for local debugging).
- Create a signing certificate and provisioning profile, or use **Automatic Provisioning** in your IDE/project settings.

## 7. End-to-end verification

```bash
# Create a new project
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for both platforms
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Run on the Android emulator
dotnet build -t:Run -f net8.0-android

# Run on the iOS simulator
dotnet build -t:Run -f net8.0-ios -p:_DeviceName=:v2:udid=<SIMULATOR_UDID>
```

Replace `net8.0` with `net9.0` etc. to match your SDK. Use `xcrun simctl list devices available` to find simulator UDIDs.

## Quick version-compatibility cheat sheet

| .NET SDK | MAUI Version | Min Xcode |
|---|---|---|
| .NET 8 LTS | MAUI 8.x | Xcode 15+ |
| .NET 9 STS | MAUI 9.x | Xcode 16+ |

Always cross-reference the exact minimum at the [MAUI release notes](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/whats-new/) before installing.

Let me provide a thorough but well-organized response covering all the prerequisites.
